meta.title: Consumer GPU/VRAM Prices vs Q4 2025 Baseline (5yr)
meta.question: At Q4 2030, how will the inflation-adjusted price of a reference consumer GPU basket compare to its Q4 2025 baseline? Intermediate checkpoints at Q4 2026 and Q4 2027 update the trajectory.
meta.resolution: BLS CPI-U-deflated mean retail price of (a) NVIDIA flagship consumer GPU MSRP and (b) median street price for $500-$800 mid-tier consumer GPU class, vs Q4 2025 baseline. Intermediate readings fire as per_event_member indicators.

priorJustifications.H1: Sustained premium reflects scenario where AI demand outpaces capacity buildouts through 2030.
priorJustifications.H2: Rough parity reflects capacity catching up to demand by 2030 — consensus expectation.
priorJustifications.H3: Modest decline reflects capacity overshooting demand if AI demand normalizes earlier.
priorJustifications.H4: Substantial catch-up reflects paradigm shift (efficient inference, alternate hardware, demand collapse); tail risk.

meta.structuralNote: H1 upper tail compressed (+15% to +∞ in one bucket). Midpoint raised from +30% to +40% to reflect AI-shock right-skew. Revisit at Q4 2026 checkpoint — split H1 if upside trajectory materializes (>+50% real).
redTeamGapsAddressed: Used GPU 5-7yr post-peak: 1080 Ti $150-200 vs $700 MSRP — strong analog for H3/H4 by 2030
HBM/DRAM supply inflection 2027-Q4 earliest; oversupply scenario in 2027-28 plausible
AMD MI500 (2027) + Intel roadmap supports alt-accelerator share growth toward 15%
DeepSeek V4 73% per-token FLOPs reduction Q1 2026 — major inference efficiency breakthrough
Q1 2026 DRAM contract +55-60% QoQ — sustained tightness through 2027 H1
meta.priorRevisionRationale: Round 2 anchored H3/H4 trajectories with: (a) 1080 Ti precedent showing real-price decline 5-7yr post-peak (analog for H3/H4); (b) Memory supply inflection 2027-2028; (c) AMD/Intel roadmap supports alt-accelerator threshold. Net: H1 down 10pp (current premium less likely to sustain to 2030), H2 up 2pp, H3 up 5pp, H4 up 3pp. By 2030: H3 modal as memory glut + alt-accelerators + efficiency gains compound.
meta.lensSetReason: Initial lens set during topic creation per skills/news-scan.md mapping

issues: Unfalsifiable hypotheses: H1, H2, H3, H4
Near-maximum uncertainty — model is not discriminating

blockers: NON-UNIFORM PRIORS without justification — posteriors are {'H1': 0.2, 'H2': 0.3, 'H3': 0.32, 'H4': 0.18} but posteriorHistory has no initial note explaining the asymmetry. Add a posteriorHistory entry documenting why.
warnings: meta.resolution may not be objectively observable — does it describe something a third party could verify?
No anti-indicators defined — every hypothesis needs at least one indicator that would REDUCE its probability
INDISTINGUISHABLE: H3 and H4 share 13/13 indicators (overlap 100%) with 0 directional discriminators — consider merging or adding an indicator that moves them in opposite directions

_design_rules: Each indicator must be OBSERVABLE — actions, not opinions
Each must have a clear fired/not-fired threshold
Every hypothesis needs at least one pro AND one anti indicator
Governor flags unfalsifiable hypotheses as DEGRADED

tier1_critical.desc: Q4 2027 real GPU basket index >=+15% vs Q4 2025 (premium persistence into year 2). Source: BLS + retail trackers.
Q4 2027 real GPU basket index sits in -15% to +15% band vs Q4 2025 (parity trajectory).
Q4 2027 real GPU basket index <=-30% vs Q4 2025 (substantial real-price decline by year 2).
Q4 2026 real GPU basket index >=+15% vs Q4 2025 (Q4 2026 measurement — per_event_member with t1_q4_2026_real_below_minus15).
Q4 2026 real GPU basket index <=-15% vs Q4 2025 baseline (early capacity catch-up). Source: BLS + retail trackers.

tier2_strong.desc: TSMC public capacity disclosure shows N2+N3 monthly wafer starts >=2x Q4 2025 by end Q4 2027. Source: TSMC quarterly earnings + TrendForce.
Combined Q4 hyperscaler (MSFT+GOOG+AMZN+META) AI infra capex prints <=YoY -5% for any FY 2026-2029. Source: company 10-Ks + Bloomberg breakouts.
Combined hyperscaler AI infra capex YoY >=+25% in 3 of 4 years 2026-2029 (REV4: softened from 'every year' resolution-disguise; can be evaluated rolling). Source: company 10-Ks.

US Commerce Dept formally relaxes BIS rule on consumer-class GPU exports to China (or equivalent rollback) before 2030. LR cap reduced in REV4 — first-order effect is bullish via Chinese demand redirect. Source: BIS.

Non-NVIDIA inference accelerators (AMD Instinct + Intel Gaudi + custom ASIC like TPU/Trainium when sold externally) reach >=15% of inference compute share globally by Q4 2028. Source: IDC accelerator tracker.
NVIDIA gross margin compresses to 55-65% range for any 2 consecutive fiscal quarters by FY2028 (parity = competitive normalization without supply collapse). Source: NVDA 10-Q.
Used/refurbished consumer GPU market index >=-25% real vs Q4 2025 by Q4 2028 (modest decline mechanism distinct from primary index check). Source: eBay sold-listings + Newegg refurb tracker.

tier3_suggestive.desc: Published peer-reviewed paper or major-lab disclosure demonstrating >=10x reduction in inference FLOPs per equivalent-quality token vs Q4 2025 baseline. LR cap reduced REV4 — multi-step inference to consumer pricing. Source: arXiv / NeurIPS / ICLR.

anti_indicators.desc: NVIDIA flagship consumer GPU MSRP rises >=20% nominal at any product refresh between 2025 and 2030. Source: NVIDIA product launch announcements.
Aggregate inference compute demand (token-volume proxy across major cloud providers) doubles YoY in any year 2026-2027 as reported in IDC/a16z/Sequoia infra updates.

actorModel.description: Real consumer GPU basket index at Q4 2030 emerges from: NVIDIA pricing power vs alternative-accelerator entry, TSMC capacity expansion, hyperscaler capex trajectory, China export-control regime, and inference-efficiency progress.

nvidia.name: NVIDIA
nvidia.role: Sets reference flagship MSRP. Pricing power constrained only by supply (TSMC) and substitute viability (AMD/Intel/ASIC).
nvidia.decisionStyle: Aggressive on margin; willing to gate consumer SKU supply to favor datacenter.
biases: Underweight long-tail substitution by hyperscaler in-house silicon
filters: Datacenter demand, TSMC allocation, channel inventory
overrides: Antitrust action OR major hyperscaler defection forces pricing reset

tsmc.name: TSMC (manufacturing supply)
tsmc.role: Sole leading-edge fab with sufficient capacity. N2/N3 monthly wafer starts cap total system supply.
tsmc.decisionStyle: Cautious on capex; long lead times on capacity decisions; geographic diversification reluctant.
biases: Anchored to customer-mix stability (Apple + NVIDIA dominate)
Taiwan geopolitical risk underweighted in capex pacing

hyperscalers.name: Hyperscalers (MSFT, GOOG, AMZN, META) — capex side
hyperscalers.role: Largest GPU buyers. Combined capex trajectory determines whether NVIDIA premium sustains or compresses.
hyperscalers.decisionStyle: Capacity-arms-race driven through 2026; potential pivot if inference revenue lags capex.
biases: Slow to throttle capex even as ROI questioned
filters: AI revenue growth, training/inference demand, custom-silicon roadmaps
overrides: Earnings miss attributed to AI capex forces visible deceleration

alt_accelerators.name: Alt-Accelerator Vendors (AMD Instinct, Intel Gaudi, Trainium/TPU when external)
alt_accelerators.role: Combined market share crossing ~15% would meaningfully erode NVIDIA premium.
alt_accelerators.decisionStyle: Slowly catching up on software ecosystem; varying execution quality.
filters: Customer wins, software stack maturity, MLPerf benchmarks

us_china_policy.name: US BIS + Chinese Cyberspace Admin
us_china_policy.role: Export-control regime determines whether Chinese demand redirects GPU supply or stays disjoint.
us_china_policy.decisionStyle: Tightening trajectory under Biden; uncertain under Trump (could relax for trade-deal leverage).
biases: Anchored to AI-race national security framing
filters: Geopolitical posture, US-China trade negotiations, Taiwan tensions
overrides: Major Taiwan crisis forces broad export-control rollback or expansion

methodology: ACTIONS OVER RHETORIC — NVIDIA earnings call commentary is RHETORIC; product launch MSRP is EVENT; BLS basket index is DATA.
Q4 CHECKPOINTS PER_EVENT_MEMBER — Q4 2026 and Q4 2027 each have mutually-exclusive bands. Only one fires per measurement; engine enforces via per_event_member shape.

AI-DEMAND-SUPPLY INFLECTION CLUSTER — TSMC, hyperscaler-capex-negative, alt-inference-share, and efficiency-breakthrough share causal_event_id=ai_supply_demand_inflection. One macro inflection should not triple-update.
CONJUNCTION SOFTENING — t2_hyperscaler_capex_above_25 was 'every year 2026-2029'; REV4 softened to '3 of 4 years' — observable mid-horizon, not resolution-disguised.
CHINA POLICY AMBIGUITY — Export-control relaxation has ambiguous first-order effect (redirects vs frees domestic supply). LR magnitude capped accordingly in REV4.

bls_cpi_u.label: BLS CPI-U (deflator for real GPU basket)
bls_cpi_u.note: Used to deflate retail GPU prices to real terms vs Q4 2025 baseline.

retail_gpu_trackers.label: Consumer GPU retail price trackers (PCPartPicker, Newegg)
retail_gpu_trackers.note: Q4 2026 + Q4 2027 real-index measurements. Per_event_member partitions.

nvidia_msrp_announcements.label: NVIDIA flagship consumer GPU MSRP at product launches
nvidia_msrp_announcements.note: anti_nvidia_pricing_power_extends. Public MSRP only — channel/scalper prices excluded.

tsmc_capacity_disclosure.label: TSMC quarterly earnings + TrendForce capacity tracking (N2/N3 wafer starts)

hyperscaler_capex_10k.label: Combined MSFT+GOOG+AMZN+META AI infra capex (10-K segments + Bloomberg breakouts)
hyperscaler_capex_10k.note: t2_hyperscaler_capex_yoy_negative + t2_hyperscaler_capex_above_25_3of4 (softened in REV4 from 'every year'). Negative leg shares ai_supply_demand_inflection causal_event_id.

bis_export_controls.label: BIS Federal Register notices on consumer-class GPU export controls to China
bis_export_controls.note: t2_china_export_controls_relaxed. LR cap reduced in REV4 (ambiguous first-order effect).

idc_accelerator_tracker.label: IDC accelerator tracker (alt-NVIDIA inference compute share)

arxiv_neurips_iclr.label: arXiv preprints + NeurIPS/ICLR proceedings on inference efficiency

ebay_newegg_refurb.label: eBay sold-listings + Newegg refurb tracker (used GPU market index)

ai_demand_reports.label: Aggregate inference compute demand (a16z + Sequoia + IDC)
ai_demand_reports.source: a16z + Sequoia + IDC AI infra reports
